baseline.evidence: Skill-less haiku run 2026-06-12, scratch _scratch/red-baseline-index/. The unaided agent's Google index-submission is built on stale/ineffective mechanisms: (I-F1) it submits the sitemap by curling the DEAD endpoint 'https://www.google.com/ping?sitemap=https://timecard.quicktools.app/sitemap.xml' — Google removed the sitemaps ping endpoint; this is a no-op. (I-F2) it includes an IndexNow POST to api.indexnow.org as a 'supplementary' Google submission step while itself noting 'Google does not use IndexNow' — a wasted/misleading channel for Google. (I-F3) per-URL indexing is a manual to-do ('go to Search Console, inspect each URL, click Request Indexing') with no recorded result per URL — a plan, not a record. (I-F4) the verdict is 'Yes, the site IS submitted to Google IF all documented steps are executed' — conflating an unexecuted plan with a completed submission. (I-F5) its SUBMISSION_RECORD.json records intended endpoints/commands (including the dead ping command) and is explicitly marked 'real_api_calls_made: false' and 'submission_type: unaided_baseline' — a plan serialized as JSON, with no GSC verification confirmation and no per-URL status. It hedged that the ping endpoint 'is old and may have changed or been superseded.'

engine_selftest.evidence:
$ python3 scripts/index_submit_gate.py --selftest (from index-submit/)
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 21 bad, 1 invariant)

skill_md.evidence: Filled skill-md-template.md: name=index-submit matches directory; description starts 'Use when' with trigger phrases 'submit the site to Google', 'get this indexed', 'verify Search Console + submit the sitemap', 'request indexing for the new URLs'; ends with 'Do not use for paid search/SEM campaigns, Bing-only indexing workflows, writing site content, or non-Google search engines.' Third person, no workflow summary. IRON LAWS: 6 laws contiguous 1-6, all traced to law_origins. Overview quotes verbatim baseline failures: dead ping endpoint URL, 'Google does not use IndexNow', manual to-do with no recorded result per URL, real_api_calls_made: false, submission_type: unaided_baseline. Rationalization table: 6 rows quoting baseline excuses. Red flags: 6 observable states with stage pointers.

evals.evidence: evals/evals.json: skill='index-submit' matches dir; notes >= 200 chars with 'baseline' present (notes = 912 chars); 6 evals, one per IRON LAW; laws 1-6 each cited in asserts: Law 1 in eval[0] (IRON LAW 1), Law 2 in eval[1] (IRON LAW 2), Law 3 in eval[2] (IRON LAW 3), Law 4 in eval[3] (IRON LAW 4), Law 5 in eval[4] (IRON LAW 5), Law 6 in eval[5] (IRON LAW 6); triggers block has 8 should_trigger and 8 should_not_trigger; all eval prompts >= 20 chars; all evals have >= 3 asserts.

self_gate.evidence:
$ python3 skill-forge/scripts/skill_gate.py index-submit (from skills/)
SKILL GATE: PASS (6 laws, package + ledger verified)

green_check.evidence:
Wrote correct executed submission-record.json at /tmp/green-verify-index-submission-record.json. $ python3 scripts/index_submit_gate.py /tmp/green-verify-index-submission-record.json
INDEX SUBMIT GATE RESULT: PASS
Re-verified after REWORK fixes (BLOCKING: _is_safe_annotation deleted + indexnow substring scan; PATCH: host-consistency checks in I3+I5; NIT: limitation note added to SKILL.md). gsc.property https://timecard.quicktools.app, sitemap + all urls on timecard.quicktools.app — hosts match.

law_origins.note: baseline I-F1: agent curled the dead google.com/ping?sitemap= endpoint and recorded it as a successful sitemap submission — Google removed that endpoint; the law requires sitemap submission via Search Console or Sitemaps API only

baseline I-F2: agent included an IndexNow POST to api.indexnow.org as a supplementary Google submission step while itself noting 'Google does not use IndexNow' — a wasted/misleading channel; the law bans IndexNow from any Google submission record field

baseline I-F3: per-URL indexing was a manual to-do ('go to Search Console, inspect each URL, click Request Indexing') with no recorded result per URL — a plan, not a record; the law requires completed indexing requests recorded with done-status per URL

baseline I-F4/I-F5: verdict was 'submitted IF steps are executed'; SUBMISSION_RECORD.json marked real_api_calls_made: false and submission_type: unaided_baseline — a plan serialized as JSON; the law requires the record to reflect only executed results

baseline I-F5: SUBMISSION_RECORD.json had no GSC verification confirmation and no gsc.verified field — indexing without a verified GSC property is not a real GSC submission; the law requires gsc.verified=true with a known verification method

house-standard fail-closed engine with selftest: the gate is non-negotiable; a verdict issued without running index_submit_gate.py and pasting literal output is a violation; counters the baseline's plan-as-record failure mode

findings: scripts/index_submit_gate.py:173: 🔴 BLOCKING: the I4 _is_safe_annotation heuristic is both too loose and too strict — live-probed. FALSE-PASS: a structured IndexNow Google submission wrapped under a key named note/notes/skipped/excluded/bing_only/etc. is exempted because the safe-key branch does `continue` (skip) WITHOUT recursing into the value, so {"notes": {"method": "indexnow", "endpoint": "api.indexnow.org", "purpose": "submit to Google"}} passes (exit 0) — the documented baseline failure (IndexNow-as-Google) slips through if mislabeled. FALSE-FAIL: a legitimate Bing-only note under any NON-magic key (e.g. {"bing_note": "...Google ignores IndexNow."}) is rejected (exit 1). The forge's claim that the wrapped case 'recurses and is refused' is incorrect. Fix: delete the entire _is_safe_annotation heuristic — this is a GOOGLE index-submission record, so IndexNow has no legitimate place in it; FAIL if 'indexnow' appears anywhere in the serialized record, using the same simple substring scan as DEAD_PING_MARKERS. Keep a bad fixture for the indexnow-as-google case and add one with indexnow under a 'notes' key proving it now fails. Update the I4/Law-2 text in SKILL.md to 'IndexNow does not belong in a Google submission record at all (it is Bing/Yandex only); record Bing submissions separately.

scripts/index_submit_gate.py:129: 🟡 PATCH: neither sitemap.url nor the per-URL entries are verified to belong to the verified gsc.property host — I3 checks only endswith('sitemap.xml'), so a sitemap or URLs on a domain OTHER than the one verified in I2 would pass (you could record a submission for a domain you do not own). Fix: assert the sitemap.url host and every urls[].url host equal the gsc.property host (strip scheme and the 'sc-domain:' prefix), and add a bad fixture with a sitemap on a different domain.

scripts/index_submit_gate.py:92: 🔵 NIT: artifact-trust ceiling — the gate validates the record's self-declarations only; a fabricated verified:true with a real-looking property, or fabricated per-URL statuses, passes I2/I5. Same inherent limit as qa-gate-runner and ship; the skill body's real-execution requirement plus reviewer/human spot-check are the mitigation.

adversarial.loophole_tried: bury the dead 'google.com/ping?sitemap=' endpoint in a nested value instead of the sitemap.method field
wrap a real IndexNow Google submission (method+endpoint+purpose) under a key named 'notes' so the safe-key exemption skips it
record per-URL entries with status 'planned' instead of a completed status
ship the record with submission_type 'unaided_baseline' or real_api_calls_made false
set gsc.verified true on a placeholder ('TODO') or fabricated-but-real-looking property
issue a 'submitted' verdict without running index_submit_gate.py

findings: scripts/index_submit_gate.py:92: 🔵 NIT: artifact-trust ceiling (carry-forward) — the gate validates the record's self-declarations; a fabricated gsc.verified true with a real-looking property, or fabricated per-URL statuses, passes I2/I5. Same inherent limit as qa-gate-runner and ship; the skill body's real-execution requirement plus a human/reviewer spot-check are the mitigation, now acknowledged in SKILL.md.
